notes:
cửa means “door.” It’s the physical door of a house, room, shop, etc.

It’s a noun. Use classifiers like cái (một cái cửa) or count directly in casual speech. Very common verb pairings: mở cửa (open the door), đóng cửa (close the door), khóa cửa (lock the door).

It can also mean “entrance” or “exit” depending on context, not just the door itself.

Related word: cổng = gate (usually bigger, outside a house or building).
exampleSentences: Anh có thể mở cửa giúp em được không?
Chị nhớ đóng cửa khi ra ngoài nhé.
Em thấy có ai đứng trước cửa nhà mình lúc nãy.

definitions.vi: Một rào cản di động được sử dụng để đóng một lối vào.
definitions.es: Una barrera móvil utilizada para cerrar una entrada.
definitions.en: A movable barrier used to close off an entrance.

example.es: La puerta de mi casa siempre está abierta.
example.en: The door of my house is always open.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you open the door for me?
Remember to close the door when you go out.
I saw someone standing in front of the door of our house earlier.

It’s a noun. Use classifiers like **cái** (một cái cửa) or count directly in casual speech. Very common verb pairings: **mở cửa** (open the door), **đóng cửa** (close the door), **khóa cửa** (lock the door).

It can also mean “entrance” or “exit” depending on context, not just the door itself.

Related word: **cổng** = gate (usually bigger, outside a house or building).

translation.en: They are standing in front of the door.
